name: Homepage loads with hero, install command, and problem comparison cards visible
description: Verifies the AI Mind Map landing page renders its core content: the navbar with logo, the hero headline and install command box, the before/after token comparison cards showing the problem being solved, and the footer.

planSteps.description: Navigate to the AI Mind Map website homepage
Verify the navigation bar is visible at the top with a logo containing 'AI Mind Map'
Verify the hero section shows a large headline and a code block containing 'npx ai-mind-map install'
Click the copy button next to the install command
Verify two side-by-side comparison cards are visible — one labeled 'Without AI Mind Map' showing high token counts, and one labeled 'With AI Mind Map' showing drastically lower token counts
Scroll down past the comparison cards to the Live Brain section
Verify the 'Live Brain' or 'Codebase Has a Brain' section heading is visible with a graph container below it
Scroll to the bottom of the page
Verify the footer is visible with links to GitHub and npm

name: Live brain graph renders real codebase nodes and responds to search and tab interactions
description: Tests the embedded D3.js force-directed knowledge graph on the landing page. Verifies it loads with real ai-mind-map codebase data (120 nodes, 300 edges), the Files/Types/Hot tabs switch content, node search filters the graph, and the Hotspots button highlights the most connected nodes.

planSteps.description: Navigate to the AI Mind Map homepage
Scroll down to the interactive brain graph section labeled 'Live Demo' or 'Codebase Has a Brain'
Verify the graph canvas is rendered and displays node count showing '120 nodes' and edge count showing '300 edges' in the stats bar
Verify circular nodes are visible inside the SVG graph area, colored differently by type
Type 'Knowledge' into the search input in the left panel
Verify the graph dims most nodes and highlights only nodes whose names match 'Knowledge', with the matching nodes remaining bright
Clear the search input
Click the 'Types' tab in the left panel
Verify the left panel now lists node type categories such as 'class', 'method', 'function' each with a count badge
Click the '🔥 Hot' tab in the left panel
Verify the left panel shows a ranked list of the most-connected nodes with degree counts visible
Click the '🔥 Hotspots' button above the graph
Verify the graph highlights the top connected nodes while dimming the rest, and the Hotspots button changes its appearance to show active state
Click the 'Clear' button
Verify the graph returns all nodes to full visibility with no selection active

name: Tools explorer page displays all MCP tools and search filtering works correctly
description: Tests the tools.html page listing all 50+ MCP tools. Verifies tools are grouped by category, each card shows a tool name and description, and the search input filters the visible tool cards in real time.

planSteps.description: Navigate to the Tools page at /tools.html
Verify the page heading references 'Tools' or 'MCP Tools' and multiple tool cards are visible on the page
Verify at least one tool card mentions 'session' or 'search' or 'digest' in its name or description
Type 'digest' into the search box
Verify the displayed tool cards are filtered to show only tools whose name or description contains 'digest', and unrelated tools are hidden
Clear the search box
Verify all tool cards reappear after clearing the search
Click on a category filter tab or button if present
Verify clicking a category shows only tools belonging to that category

name: Token savings calculator updates dynamically when sliders are adjusted
description: Tests the calculator.html interactive token savings calculator. Verifies the page loads with visible sliders and a savings display, and that adjusting a slider causes the displayed savings percentage and token counts to update dynamically without page reload.

planSteps.description: Navigate to the Token Calculator page at /calculator.html
Verify the page shows a calculator heading and at least one interactive slider control
Verify an initial savings value or percentage is displayed on the page (e.g. showing '80%' or '99%' token savings)
Drag or interact with the first slider to change its value
Verify the displayed token savings amount or percentage updates to reflect the new slider value, showing a different number than before
Verify no error messages or 'NaN' or 'undefined' appear in the savings output after the slider interaction

name: Install wizard shows correct setup command when different AI agents are selected
description: Tests the install.html agent selection wizard. Verifies agent buttons (Claude, Cursor, VS Code Copilot, etc.) are present, clicking each updates the displayed install command to match that agent's specific setup instructions, and the copy button is functional.

planSteps.description: Navigate to the Install page at /install.html
Verify the page shows an install guide heading and multiple agent selection buttons including at least Claude and Cursor
Verify a code block with an install command is visible on the page
Click the button for the Claude agent
Verify the install command updates to show the Claude-specific configuration or mentions 'claude' in the command or config snippet
Click the button for the Cursor agent
Verify the install command updates and now shows Cursor-specific configuration, different from the Claude command
Click the copy button for the install command
Verify the copy button gives visual feedback (e.g. changes text to 'Copied!' or shows a checkmark) confirming the command was copied

name: Website is fully responsive on mobile viewport with hamburger menu working
description: Tests that the landing page layout adapts correctly to a 375px mobile viewport. Verifies desktop navigation links are hidden and replaced by a hamburger menu icon, tapping the hamburger opens the mobile menu, and there is no horizontal overflow on the page.

planSteps.description: Navigate to the AI Mind Map homepage
Resize the browser viewport to 375px wide (mobile iPhone SE size)
Verify the desktop navigation links are no longer visible and a hamburger menu icon (three lines) appears in the navbar instead
Click the hamburger menu icon
Verify a mobile navigation menu slides open or appears, showing navigation links for pages like Home, Explorer, Tools, Calculator, Install
Click a navigation link inside the mobile menu such as 'Tools'
Verify the page navigates to the Tools page and the mobile menu closes
Navigate back to the homepage
Verify the hero headline and install command are visible and readable at mobile width without requiring horizontal scrolling
